steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice 'emphasizes a difference between the two birds.' A valid answer must mention both birds and frame the information as a contrast, not merely a shared trait or a description of only one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Though they share several traits in common, the long-billed starthroat is larger than the ruby-topaz hummingbird.: uses a concessive structure to acknowledge similarity while highlighting a size difference between the two birds. Both birds appear and a contrast is drawn. ✓
  • Choice B — Identifiable by its very long, black, straight bill and its turquoise crown, the long-billed starthroat is a large hummingbird found in Suriname.: describes only one of the two birds; the other is not mentioned. ✗
  • Choice C — The long-billed starthroat and the ruby-topaz hummingbird are two of the thirty-five hummingbird species found in Suriname.: mentions both birds but focuses on what they share, not a difference. ✗
  • Choice D — The ruby-topaz hummingbird is a small hummingbird identifiable by its crimson crown and its short, black, curved bill.: describes only one of the two birds. ✗
That matches Choice A — Though they share several traits in common, the long-billed starthroat is larger than the ruby-topaz hummingbird..
